title: Rahm Takes Mexico City: Six-Shot Masterclass, Twin Trophies for Legion XIII
excerpt: Jon Rahm's dominant six-shot victory at LIV Golf Mexico City makes him the league's winningest player, with Legion XIII claiming both individual and team titles.
body:
Rahm's Mexican Masterpiece

Jon Rahm arrived at Club de Golf Chapultepec on the back of a disappointing tie for 38th at the Masters, but you would never have known it on Sunday. The Legion XIII captain delivered a four-hole demolition that included three birdies and an eagle, ultimately firing a bogey-free 7-under 64 to claim his most decisive victory yet in LIV Golf — a commanding six-shot win over young David Puig of Fireballs GC.

Winning Score
-21
Rahm LIV Titles
4
Career LIV Trophies
16
Legion XIII Team Win
-45

The Sunday Clinic

Opening with a par, Rahm drove the 318-yard par-4 second for an easy birdie from 66 feet away. He followed by driving the 390-yard par-4 third, his brilliant tee shot finding the heart of the green inside 3 feet for an eagle. Birdie putts from 14 and 20 feet on holes four and five extended his lead to six before most of the field had even turned for home.

Meanwhile, his Legion XIII teammate Tyrrell Hatton, who started the day just two shots back in the final group, suffered a double bogey at the drivable second and never recovered. Puig, however, kept the heat on with birdies in three of his first four holes and an eagle at the par-5 seventh, pulling within three. But when Rahm drained a crucial 15-foot par save at the 10th — his most important shot of the day — and followed with a birdie at the par-5 12th, the contest was effectively over.

Spanish Dominance on the Podium

In a stunning show of Spanish golf supremacy, Rahm, Puig and Josele Ballester swept the entire podium. Puig's runner-up finish at -15 marks his best result since joining LIV Golf, and the 24-year-old showed maturity beyond his years. Ballester, 22, secured solo third at -14 for his second career podium finish, with both young stars training regularly with Rahm in Arizona.

"I spend more time with him than I do with my wife or my kids," Rahm quipped about Puig, offering a glimpse into their tight friendship and competitive atmosphere.

Rahm Now The League Leader

With his Mexico City victory, Rahm has now celebrated 16 LIV Golf trophies in just three seasons — surpassing Bryson DeChambeau's 15 and establishing himself as the league's most prolific winner. The tally includes 4 tournament titles, 2 Individual Championships, 9 team tournament wins, and 1 Team Championship. His Legion XIII squad also won the team competition by nine shots, moving to a 9-9 record with the Crushers for most regular-season team titles.

The Road to USGA Exemptions

Thomas Detry of 4Aces GC sits in pole position for the U.S. Open exemption reserved for the top non-exempt player in the top 3 of LIV's season-long Individual standings after May 18. Detry's tie for fifth moved him to third overall, with Rahm and DeChambeau already exempt from majors.
